title: Firmware: implement spec §18.2 position resources (/sensors/location, /pos, /pos/cache) in SenML+CBOR
description:
The Rust client now has a spec-faithful SenML Position codec (lichen-client pos.rs, commit ca03c292; spec 12-apps §18.2), and lichen-cli 'position show' targets /sensors/location. But NO firmware node exposes these resources yet — only the gateway's binary /inbound/location POST (inbound_coap.c, a versioned binary format, NOT SenML) exists. To make position sharing actually work end-to-end:
- GET /sensors/location -> application/senml+cbor {bn=urn:dev:mac:<eui>:, bt, lat/lon/alt/speed/heading} (§18.2.2)
- PUT /pos (beacon, §18.2.1) + GET /pos/cache {positions:[{node,lat,lon,alt,ts,age_s}]} (CBOR, not SenML)
Zephyr has no SenML lib; the C side would need a small SenML+CBOR encoder (or reuse zcbor with the RFC 8428 integer labels bn=-2,bt=-3,n=0,u=1,v=2). Until this lands, the client position codec is validated by its own unit tests only (no live node). Consider whether to also expose the existing binary /inbound/location as a client op vs standardizing on SenML.
